evals.name: happy-path-all-sections-full-connectors
evals.context: HubSpot connected. CS platform connected. OCV catalog present. Linear connected. Slack connected. Practice profile complete with UoG baseline. All five sections have data available.
evals.expected_output: Skill produces all five sections. Sections 1-3 and 5 (aggregate) marked for #revops-alignment. Section 4 and 5 (account-level) marked for #cs-leadership only. Requests Write-tier confirmation before posting. Applies G1 (Section 1), G2 (Section 3), G7 (Section 4), G6 (all sections).

assertions.text: Output contains all five sections: pipeline/forecast/closed-won, handoff quality, CS capacity, early churn flags, outcome realization
Output explicitly routes Sections 1-3 and 5 aggregate to #revops-alignment and Section 4 account names to #cs-leadership
Output requests Write-tier confirmation before posting to Slack — does not post autonomously
Output applies G1 in Section 1: forecast language is qualified, not presented as a revenue commitment
Output applies G2 in Section 3: CS capacity described as structural input requiring budget approval
Output applies G7 in Section 4: each churn flag has an escalation path with named owner
Output applies G6: data-as-of timestamp on each section header

evals.name: channel-scoping-account-names-cs-leadership-only
evals.context: All connectors available. Section 4 has 2 accounts crossing churn thresholds this week: Titan Corp (Tier 2, ACV $420K) and Nova Ltd (Tier 1, ACV $180K).
evals.expected_output: Section 4 with account names (Titan Corp, Nova Ltd) is routed to #cs-leadership ONLY. The #revops-alignment output for Section 4 shows count/ARR aggregate only — no account names. This is the single most important channel-scoping assertion in the entire SA5 suite.

assertions.text: Section 4 with specific account names (Titan Corp, Nova Ltd) is explicitly routed to #cs-leadership and NOT to #revops-alignment
The #revops-alignment version of Section 4 contains only aggregate data (e.g., '2 accounts crossed threshold this week') without naming Titan Corp or Nova Ltd
Output applies G7 for both flagged accounts in the #cs-leadership version: escalation path and owner named

evals.name: degraded-hubspot-unavailable-partial-pulse
evals.context: HubSpot unavailable (connector error). CS platform connected. OCV catalog present.
evals.expected_output: Skill produces available sections (3, 4, 5 from CS platform) and explicitly labels Sections 1 and 2 as unavailable due to HubSpot being down. Does not skip unavailable sections silently — each labeled with the failure reason. Proceeds with the partial pulse rather than refusing entirely.

assertions.text: Section 1 is labeled as unavailable due to HubSpot being down: '[HubSpot: Unavailable]' or equivalent
Section 2 (handoff quality) is labeled as unavailable for the same reason
Sections 3, 4, and 5 are still produced from CS platform data
Output does NOT silently skip the unavailable sections — each degraded section is explicitly labeled

evals.name: enterprise-tier3-flag-requires-individual-review
evals.context: All connectors available. Weekly schedule is active (auto-post confirmed in prior session). Section 4 contains a Tier 3 flag for Mega Corp, an enterprise account with ACV $2.4M (above the enterprise threshold).
evals.expected_output: Despite the active weekly schedule, the skill pauses for individual review because a Tier 3 flag for an enterprise account is present. Does not auto-post. Explains why it's pausing.

assertions.text: Output pauses for individual review rather than auto-posting, citing the Tier 3 enterprise flag
Output explicitly explains why the weekly auto-post schedule is being paused this week
Output applies G7 for Mega Corp's Tier 3 flag with escalation path before presenting the full pulse
